Home»Elon Musk calls for Christians to ‘stand up’ against Olympics show

Elon Musk has called on Christians to be ‘brave to stand up for what is fair and right’, amid the Olympics Opening Ceremony scandal.

The world’s richest man, Elon Musk, is one of the biggest and loudest critics of the Paris Olympics Opening Ceremony, which he believes was “disrespectful to all Christians.”

Musk – who was born in Pretoria, South Africa – has also chastised Christians for allowing their faith to be publicly desecrated at the global sporting event.

The performance has beencriticised for parodying the Last Supper painting,which represents Jesus and his 12 disciples.

On his official account, the X founder criticised the Paris Olympics Opening Ceremony on Friday, 26 July.

“This was extremely disrespectful to Christians,” he tweeted.

The businessman also retweeted messages claiming that the Olympics woulddare not insult other religions.

When one tweep posted a side-by-side of The Last Supper and the Opening Ceremony with the words: “Doing this results in fewer dead people than if the imagery of Islam was mocked,” Elon responded: “Christianity has become toothless.”

He added in another tweet: “Unless there is more bravery to stand up for what is fair and right, Christianity will perish.”

When on tweep questioned about his religious standing, Elon Musk responded: “I believe in the principles of Christianity like love thy neighbor as thyself (have empathy for all) and turn the other cheek (end the cycle of retribution)”.

Meanwhile, outspoken social media personality Andrew Tate has also condemned the visuals of the Olympics Opening Ceremony.

He’s even gone as far as to call on others to boycott the global sporting event.

In a video post, he added, “The worst part is that none of this has anything to do with sports. They could’ve done this at any other time, but no, they chose this random event”.

Andrew – who is based in Romania –  also posted a clip of him protesting outside the French embassy.

He tweeted: “I am protesting outside of the French embassy for mocking Jesus during the Olympics ceremony.

“The police are threatening to arrest me for unlawful assembly. I am currently on bail and will face a minimum of three months in prison. I do this as a Muslim”.

He added: “WHERE ARE THE CHRISTIANS?”

title: Elon Musk calls for Christians to 'stand up' against Olympics show
author: Megan van den Heever
description: Elon Musk has called on Christians to be 'brave to stand up for what is fair and right', amid the Olympics Opening Ceremony scandal.
